solution:
def sumOfTheDigitsOfHarshadNumber(self, x: int) -> int:
    if x < 10:
        return x
    return self.sumOfTheDigitsOfHarshadNumber(int(x//10)) + x % 10

efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(log(n))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The solution uses a recursive function to check if the number is a Harshad number or not. If the number is less than 10, it is a Harshad number and the function returns the number itself. Otherwise, it divides the number by 10 recursively until the number becomes less than 10, then adds the remaining digit to the result of the recursive call. This approach has a time complexity of O(log(n)) due to the recursive calls, and a space complexity of O(1) since only a constant amount of memory is used. The energy implications are low as the function only performs basic arithmetic operations and recursion.
